L1.area: Matrix data storage and serialization layer

L2.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the deserialization logic for dynamically-sized matrix storage. The automatically derived deserialization implementation does not validate that the number of elements in the deserialized data matches the expected dimensions (rows times columns). An attacker can craft malicious serialized input where the claimed dimensions exceed the actual data length, leading to out-of-bounds memory access when the matrix is subsequently used.

L3.area: Matrix data storage and serialization layer
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the deserialization logic for dynamically-sized matrix storage. The automatically derived deserialization implementation does not validate that the number of elements in the deserialized data matches the expected dimensions (rows times columns). An attacker can craft malicious serialized input where the claimed dimensions exceed the actual data length, leading to out-of-bounds memory access when the matrix is subsequently used.

ground_truth.reason: The automatically derived Deserialize implementation for VecStorage does not validate that the number of deserialized elements equals nrows * ncols, allowing crafted inputs to cause out-of-bounds memory access.
